description: Paradigm continuity inside an agent context: after an agent design is on the table, an incremental capability request ('also Slack me when it escalates') stays a tool/capability on that agent — not a separate spawned workflow and not a reason to re-anchor the design. Mirror of ctx-followup-schedule-edit on the agent side. Ported from the intent-resolution spec (§5.9 ctx-inline-agent-add-slack), reshaped from priorConversation to a live follow-up turn. Recalibrated 2026-07-10: the original ticket-triage premise was defensibly workflow-anchored (classify-then-route), so the continuity test never ran — the premise now carries an unambiguous chat signal, matching the shape that reliably anchors on an agent in compound-weather-and-support-assistant.

conversation.text: A support assistant our customers chat with — it answers their product questions and escalates to a human when it can't help. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [After the assistant lays out its approach, ask for exactly one change: also send me a Slack message whenever it escalates to a human.
Do not approve any plan, setup card, or build confirmation.
Once the change is reflected in the approach, say you'll think it over and end the conversation.]

processExpectations: The initial proposal is agent-anchored: an n8n Agent that triages tickets and decides per ticket, not a fixed workflow pipeline.

The Slack notification request is treated as an added capability/tool of the proposed n8n Agent — the agent does not re-classify the automation, re-anchor to a workflow design, or propose a separate second automation for the notification.
